explanations.1: This sets the Python version that pre-commit will use when running hooks; it's telling the system to use Python 3.13.
explanations.16: This hook sorts all lines alphabetically in files matching the pattern, which is useful for keeping lists organized consistently.
explanations.27: The 'local' repo type means these hooks are custom scripts on your machine, not downloaded from GitHub like the others above.
explanations.34: This ensures the hook runs one file at a time instead of in parallel, which some tools need to work correctly.
explanations.41: Like line 34, this prevents the hook from running multiple files simultaneously, since mypy can behave differently in parallel mode.
